name: Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section
name_original: Barranco de la Hoz Mala – ruta de las pasarelas desde Aldehuela
description: Hike the easy 3.6-mile Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section, featuring hanging walkways, a waterfall, and the Aliaga Power Station.
region_intro: Hiking around Aliaga offers diverse natural landscapes, characterized by riverine environments, deep gorges, and picturesque waterfalls. The region features varied terrain, including forests with autochthonous flora and unique geological formations. Trails range from easy paths along rivers to more challenging routes with notable elevation gains. This area provides a compelling destination for outdoor enthusiasts seeking diverse hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Aliaga The most popular hiking route is Valloré Viewpoint Trail, a 2.4 miles (3.9 km) trail that takes about 1 hour 15 minutes to complete. This easy path offers scenic views of the surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local hikers is Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section, an easy 3.6 miles (5.7 km) path. This route follows the river through a gorge, featuring hanging walkways and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Valloré Footbridges – Valloré Footbridges loop from Antigua Central térmica de Aliaga, a 10.6 miles (17.1 km) trail leading through riverine and gorge environments, often completed in about 4 hours 49 minutes. Hiking around Aliaga is defined by riverine trails, impressive waterfalls, and varied forest environments. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more strenuous hikes. The routes in Aliaga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 550 hikers have used komoot to explore Aliaga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails in Aliaga?
A: Hiking in Aliaga offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find riverine environments with hanging walkways, deep gorges like the Hocino Gorge, and trails through forests featuring autochthonous flora such as poplars and gorse. The area also boasts unique geological formations, providing varied landscapes for exploration.

Q: Are there hiking options suitable for beginners or families in Aliaga?
A: Yes, Aliaga provides a good selection of trails for all skill levels. Out of the 29 available routes, 10 are classified as easy, making them ideal for beginners or families. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493780/vallore-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 2.4-mile path offering scenic views.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Aliaga?
A: There are over 25 hiking routes documented in Aliaga, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include 10 easy routes, 15 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging trails for experienced hikers.

Q: What is the typical duration of hikes around Aliaga?
A: Hikes in Aliaga vary significantly in duration. Shorter, easy paths like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974493780/vallore-viewpoint-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Viewpoint Trail&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 15 minutes. Moderate routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges – Valloré Footbridges loop&lt;/a&gt;, can take around 4 hours 49 minutes, while longer, more strenuous hikes might require over 3,000 feet of elevation gain and several hours to complete.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Aliaga?
A: Yes, Aliaga is home to several picturesque waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3793681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River&lt;/a&gt;, which is part of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974480574/guadalope-river-trail-hoz-mala-section" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable waterfalls in the region include the Clara Waterfall and the Tamborera Waterfall.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I discover while hiking in Aliaga?
A: Aliaga's trails lead to various natural landmarks and viewpoints. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5248074" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of several routes, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1637345" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Majalinos&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views. The region also features unique geological formations and the Incense Juniper.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Aliaga?
A: Yes, there are several circular hiking routes available. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges – Valloré Footbridges loop from Antigua Central térmica de Aliaga&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 10.6-mile trail. Another circular route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31452877" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canyon – Aliaga Power Plant loop from Aliaga&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Aliaga?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Aliaga, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 70 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse natural landscapes, particularly the riverine trails and impressive waterfalls. The varied terrain, from easy paths to more strenuous climbs, also contributes to its popularity among over 550 explorers.

Q: Are there any specific trails that follow rivers or go through gorges?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974480574/guadalope-river-trail-hoz-mala-section" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent example, featuring hanging walkways and following the river through a gorge. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valloré Footbridges – Valloré Footbridges loop&lt;/a&gt; also leads through riverine and gorge environments.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Aliaga?
A: Aliaga offers hiking opportunities throughout the year, but the best time often depends on your preference. Spring and autumn provide pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. While some waterfalls like Tamborera can freeze in winter, offering a unique sight, trails can vary in accessibility. Summer can be warm, making early morning or late afternoon hikes more comfortable.

Q: Are there any natural springs along the hiking routes for refreshment?
A: Yes, hikers can find natural springs in the Aliaga area. The Cedrilla spring, located just outside Aliaga, is one such spot where you can find refreshment during your hike.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by historical or industrial sites?
A: Yes, some trails in Aliaga offer a blend of natural beauty and historical context. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974480574/guadalope-river-trail-hoz-mala-section" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, passes by the Aliaga Power Station, providing a glimpse into the region's industrial heritage amidst the natural landscape.
tips: A nice place on the course of the Guadalope River. It can be reached by a path that runs through the Barranco de la Hoz Mala to the Fuente de la Tosca.
Quite stunning ruined power station in an amazing steep canyon. Certainly not what you'd expect to find here!

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section?
faqs.answer: While generally classified as 'easy' in terms of overall length and elevation, the Hoz Mala Section is considered to be of moderate difficulty due to its adventurous nature. It features sections assisted by chains and requires good fitness and sure-footedness. It is not recommended for those who suffer from vertigo, as it includes areas with steep walls and hanging walkways.

faqs.question: What are the main features and terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail follows the Guadalope riverbed, offering a unique experience with impressive sections of hanging walkways and steep walls. You'll traverse a landscape shaped by the river, with notable views of the surrounding mountains. Hikers will encounter varied terrain, including some paved parts, but also segments requiring careful footing and assistance from chains.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Hoz Mala Section?
faqs.answer: The trail is typically accessed from the nearby village of Aldehuela. Parking is usually available in or near the village, providing convenient access to the trailhead for the Hoz Mala Section and the famous hanging walkways.

faqs.question: Is the Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Due to the trail's challenging sections, including hanging walkways and parts assisted by chains, it is generally not recommended for dogs. The terrain can be difficult for pets to navigate safely, especially in areas with steep drops or narrow passages.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike the Hoz Mala Section is typically during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, making the hike more comfortable. Summer can be very hot, and winter might present challenges with colder temperatures and potentially icy conditions on certain sections.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Hoz Mala Section or Aliaga Geological Park?
faqs.answer: The Guadalope River Trail: Hoz Mala Section is located within the Aliaga Geological Park. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this particular trail. However, it's always advisable to check the official park website or local information centers for any updated regulations or seasonal restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll experience the dramatic river landscape and unique geological formations. Key highlights include the stunning Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River and the historic Aliaga Power Station. The hanging walkways themselves are a significant attraction, offering unique perspectives of the canyon.

faqs.question: Is the Hoz Mala Section suitable for beginners or families with young children?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's moderate difficulty, sections with chains, and exposure to steep drops, it is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. Hikers should have good physical fitness, be sure-footed, and not suffer from vertigo to safely enjoy this adventurous route.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is accessible from the village of Aldehuela, where you may find basic amenities such as small shops or cafes. For more extensive services, including accommodation or a wider selection of restaurants, you might need to visit larger nearby towns.

faqs.question: What should I wear and bring for hiking the Hoz Mala Section?
faqs.answer: You should wear sturdy hiking boots with good grip, as the terrain can be uneven and potentially slippery. Bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, and snacks. A small backpack, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and appropriate layers of clothing for changing weather conditions are also recommended. Consider bringing gloves for the chain sections if you have sensitive hands.

faqs.question: Is it possible to do a circular walk on the Hoz Mala Section?
faqs.answer: The Hoz Mala Section itself is primarily an out-and-back route along the riverbed and walkways. However, it can be combined with other local trails or paths to create longer, circular routes, often involving higher paths that offer different perspectives of the canyon. Consult local maps or komoot tours for options that create a loop.

waypoints.name: Waterfall in Barranco de la Hoz Mala on the Guadalope River
